title: Caprifolium stabianum
taxonomy: Caprifolium stabianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is commonly known as the Stabian honeysuckle or Stabian caprifig. Caprifolium stabianum belongs to the order Dipsacales and is native to certain parts of Europe. This plant is an evergreen shrub with fragrant flowers, which are typically white or pale yellow in color. Caprifolium stabianum is often found growing in woodland edges, rocky areas, and scrublands.

intro: Caprifolium stabianum, also known as the Honeysuckle vine, is a perennial climbing plant that belongs to the Caprifoliaceae family. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Caprifolium stabianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
